title: A bunch of refactoring prerequisites to #28043 + a behavior change of `--registries-conf`
body:
This a collection of refactorings conceptually a part of #28043. The CI pipeline doesn’t like too many commits in a single PR, and these are hopefully uncontroversial and a small improvement either way, so I think it would save time to merge them now, while the design of #28043 is discussed further.

Should not change behavior (apart from fixing `ocipull.pullOptions.credentials`, but that’s not reachable).

See individual commit messages for details.

#### Checklist

Ensure you have completed the following checklist for your pull request to be reviewed:
<!-- Use [x] to mark as done, or click the checkbox after opening PR -->

- [ ] Certify you wrote the patch or otherwise have the right to pass it on as an open-source patch by signing all
commits. (`git commit -s`). (If needed, use `git commit -s --amend`).  The author email must match
the sign-off email address. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs)
for more information.
- [ ] Referenced issues using `Fixes: #00000` in commit message (if applicable)
- [ ] [Tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/test#readme) have been added/updated (or no tests are needed)
- [ ] [Documentation](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/README.md) has been updated (or no documentation changes are needed)
- [ ] All commits pass `make validatepr` (format/lint checks)
- [ ] [Release note](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/release-notes.md) entered in the section below (or `None` if no user-facing changes)

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: [NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.
(For transparency, I have set “No New Tests”.)

The commit `Simplify setting up Runtime.imageContext` that causes the failure is the test’s (correct) expectation that `podman info` reports the configured `image_copy_tmp_dir`. The code was wrong before; the PR didn’t break runtime behavior, it just made this pre-existing bug visible in CI. 

Before runtime’s `tmp dir` was effectively decided inside `libimage`, when it received `nil` and filled in from config.

After the PR, the `tmp dir` is decided in `libpod` (via `parse.GetTempDir()` at runtime creation) and then handed to `libimage`. The runtime still ends up with the correct value from config; the place where it’s set just moved from `libimage when given nil` to `libpod when creating the context`.

I helped with AI to understand where the problem is.
I’m not sure if this fix should be part of the refactor or if we should create a separate PR for it. I'm happy to create the PR myself. Let me know if I should go ahead with that? @mtrmac
I think “I broke it so I should fix it” is a good general rule, to discourage leaving a mess for others to clean up  — but I’d certainly appreciate the help if you have the time.

> Before runtime’s `tmp dir` was effectively decided inside `libimage`, when it received `nil` and filled in from config.

Oh… I was staring at that for a long time and I couldn’t see anything. _That’s_ what I was missing: both versions of the code eventually set `r.imageContext.BigFilesTemporaryDir`, but the previous version initialized `libimage` before setting that field that, _except_ if `options.WithRegistriesConf` was set:

```console
# (unset TMPDIR; CONTAINERS_CONF=$(pwd)/test-storage.conf podman --registries-conf=/dev/null info  "--format" "{{.Store.ImageCopyTmpDir}}"; echo $?)
WARN[0000] Failed to retrieve default tmp dir: invalid image_copy_tmp_dir value "storage1" (relative paths are not accepted) 

0
# (unset TMPDIR; CONTAINERS_CONF=$(pwd)/test-storage.conf podman info  "--format" "{{.Store.ImageCopyTmpDir}}"; echo $?)
Error: configure storage: invalid image_copy_tmp_dir value "storage1" (relative paths are not accepted)
125
```

I.e. I was thinking I was making a clean refactor but this was really a behavior change.

Thanks for finding the cause!

I have updated the PR so that `--registries-conf` no longer affects temporary directory processing, and so that the refactor is no longer changing the `ImageCopyTmpDir` logic.

I rather think there is more to be done in this problem space — we can end up with `runtime.imageContext` and `runtime.SystemContext` returning different configurations and it’s not at all clear which one should be used when. But that’s a bit out of scope for this PR and my immediate work.
LGTM on my end. I have some concerns about the behavior change to tmpdir handling, but 6.0 is a major version so my level of concern is minimal.
